title: primula boothii subsp. autumnalis
intro: Primula boothii subsp. autumnalis, also known as the Tibetan autumnal primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the primrose family that is native to the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Primula boothii subsp. autumnalis include its ability to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral and antioxidant agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Tibetan medicine to treat various ailments.
